Package org.eventb.core.ast

Interface ISpecialization

All Superinterfaces:
Cloneable
All Known Implementing Classes:
Specialization
public interface ISpecialization extends Cloneable
Common protocol for describing a specialization, which groups together type substitutions, free-identifier substitutions, and predicate-variable substitutions in one object. It allows to specialize a formula by applying all these substitutions at once.

Instances of this class can be created only through a formula factory. Type, free-identifier and predicate-variable substitutions are then added one by one using put() methods. Finally, the instance is passed as argument to a specialize() method on a type, type environment or formula.

The substitutions can also change the formula factory. Consequently, all substitutes must have been created with the same formula factory as this specialization.

These substitutions must be compatible, that is the expression to be substituted for an identifier must bear a type which is the result of applying the type substitutions to the type of the identifier.

This condition must hold after every method call. This implies that a type substitution required for a free identifier substitution should be put in the specialization strictly before the considered free identifier substitution.

When specializing a type, type-environment or a formula, an instance of this class can change by side-effect in the following manner: For each free identifier, not already part of the free identifier substitution and which occurs in the specialized type, type environment or formula, a new identifier substitution is added to this specialization. It maps that identifier to an identifier with the same name, but bearing the type obtained by specializing the type of the original identifier.

For instance, if a specialization substitutes type T for type S and is applied to a type environment containing free identifier x of type S, then the specialization will be complemented with the substitution of x of type T for x of type S.

When defining substitutions, a specialization also maintains a destination type environment. It contains all the identifiers that have been used in the right-hand sides of substitutions, as well as all identifiers that have occurred in a formula passed to specialize() methods and that have not been substituted.

The purpose of this destination type environment, together with the automatic completion of substitutions ensures that successive applications of a specialization to several type environments or formulas are all compatible. In particular, if a formula type-checks with respect to some type environment, then the specialized formula also type-checks with the specialized type environment, under the condition that both specialization are performed with the same instance of this class.

Similarly, when specializing a formula, an instance of this class can change by side-effect in the following manner: For each predicate variable, not already part of the predicate variable substitution and which occurs in the formula, a new predicate variable substitution is added to this specialization. It maps that predicate variable to itself.

In the rare cases where the side-effects are not wanted, one can always clone a specialization object and use the clone to apply a specialization.

Since:
2.6
See Also:
  • FormulaFactory.makeSpecialization()
  • Type.specialize(ISpecialization)
  • ITypeEnvironment.specialize(ISpecialization)
  • Formula.specialize(ISpecialization)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    canPut(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
    Checks whether the proposed free-identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition of put(FreeIdentifier, Expression) is satisfied.
    boolean
    canPut(GivenType type, Expression value)
    Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for put(GivenType, Expression) is satisfied.
    boolean
    canPut(GivenType type, Type value)
    Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for put(GivenType, Type) is satisfied.
    ISpecialization
    clone()
    Returns a deep copy of this specialization.
    Expression
    get(FreeIdentifier ident)
    Returns the expression to be substituted for the given identifier.
    Type
    get(GivenType type)
    Returns the type to be substituted for the given given type.
    Predicate
    get(PredicateVariable predVar)
    Returns the predicate to be substituted for the given predicate variable.
    FormulaFactory
    getFactory()
    Returns the formula factory with which this specialization was created.
    FreeIdentifier[]
    getFreeIdentifiers()
    Returns the set of free identifiers to be substituted.
    PredicateVariable[]
    getPredicateVariables()
    Returns the set of predicate variables to be substituted.
    GivenType[]
    getTypes()
    Returns the set of given types to be substituted.
    void
    put(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
    Adds a new free identifier substitution to this specialization.
    void
    put(GivenType type, Expression value)
    Adds a new type substitution to this specialization.
    void
    put(GivenType type, Type value)
    Adds a new type substitution to this specialization.
    boolean
    put(PredicateVariable predVar, Predicate value)
    Adds a new predicate variable substitution to this specialization.

  • Method Details

    • clone

      ISpecialization clone()
      Returns a deep copy of this specialization. This method is useful to protect a specialization from side-effects when applying it to a type environment or a mathematical formula.
      Returns:
      a deep copy of this specialization

    • getFactory

      FormulaFactory getFactory()
      Returns the formula factory with which this specialization was created.
      Returns:
      the formula factory of this specialization
      Since:
      3.1

    • put

      void put(GivenType type, Type value)
      Adds a new type substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - replacement for the given type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this substitution is not compatible with already registered substitutions or the value has been created by another formula factory
      See Also:
      • Type.getFactory()
      • canPut(GivenType, Type)

    • put

      void put(GivenType type, Expression value)
      Adds a new type substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The value must be a type-checked type expression. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - typed type expression replacement for the given type
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not typed or is not a type expression, or if this substitution is not compatible with already registered substitutions or the value has been created by another formula factory
      Since:
      3.5
      See Also:
      • Type.getFactory()
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Expression.isATypeExpression()
      • canPut(GivenType, Expression)

    • get

      Type get(GivenType type)
      Returns the type to be substituted for the given given type.
      Parameters:
      type - some given type
      Returns:
      the type to be substituted for the given given type or null if no substitution has been defined for the given given type
      Since:
      3.1

    • put

      void put(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
      Adds a new free identifier substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. Both parameters must be type-checked. The given identifier must not denote a type. The added substitution must be compatible with already registered substitutions (for both given types and free identifiers). The given expression needs not be well-formed. In case the expression contains bound identifiers, it is up to the caller to manage that they do not escape their scope. The given expression must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier to substitute
      value - a typed expression to substitute for the given identifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either parameter is not typed, or if the identifier denotes a type, or if this substitution is not compatible with already registered substitutions or the value has been created by another formula factory
      See Also:
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Formula.getFactory()
      • canPut(FreeIdentifier, Expression)

    • get

      Expression get(FreeIdentifier ident)
      Returns the expression to be substituted for the given identifier.
      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier
      Returns:
      the expression to be substituted for the given identifier or null if no substitution has been defined for the given identifier
      Since:
      3.1

    • canPut

      boolean canPut(GivenType type, Type value)
      Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for put(GivenType, Type) is satisfied. The value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - replacement for the given type
      Returns:
      true if the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value has been created by another formula factory
      Since:
      3.3

    • canPut

      boolean canPut(GivenType type, Expression value)
      Checks whether the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition for put(GivenType, Expression) is satisfied. The value must by a typed type expression and must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Parameters:
      type - given type to specialize
      value - typed type expression replacement for the given type
      Returns:
      true if the proposed type substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not typed, is not a type expression or has been created by another formula factory
      Since:
      3.5

    • canPut

      boolean canPut(FreeIdentifier ident, Expression value)
      Checks whether the proposed free-identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions (for given types, free identifiers, and predicate variables), i.e., if the precondition of put(FreeIdentifier, Expression) is satisfied. Both the type and the value must be type-checked. The value expression must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance. This method does not modify the specialization instance.
      Parameters:
      ident - a typed identifier to substitute
      value - a typed expression to substitute for the given identifier
      Returns:
      true if the proposed free identifier substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions. Return false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either parameter is not typed, or the value has been created by another formula factory
      Since:
      3.3

    • put

      boolean put(PredicateVariable predVar, Predicate value)
      Adds a new predicate variable substitution to this specialization. All substitutions will be applied in parallel when specializing a formula. The given predicate value must be typed, but needs not be well-formed. In case the predicate value contains bound identifiers, it is up to the caller to manage that they do not escape their scope. The given predicate value must have been created by the same formula factory as this instance.

      This method can have side-effects, as described in ISpecialization.

      Parameters:
      predVar - a predicate variable to substitute
      value - a typed predicate to substitute for the given predicate variable.
      Returns:
      true if the proposed predicate variable substitution is compatible with already registered substitutions, and this specialization instance has been changed accordingly. Return false otherwise and this specialization instance is unchanged.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not typed or has been created by another formula factory
      Since:
      3.3
      See Also:
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()
      • Formula.getFactory()

    • get

      Predicate get(PredicateVariable predVar)
      Returns the predicate to be substituted for the given predicate variable.
      Parameters:
      predVar - a predicate variable.
      Returns:
      the predicate to be substituted for the given predicate variable or null if no substitution has been defined for the given predicate variable
      Since:
      3.3

    • getTypes

      GivenType[] getTypes()
      Returns the set of given types to be substituted.
      Returns:
      the set of given types to be substituted
      Since:
      3.3

    • getFreeIdentifiers

      FreeIdentifier[] getFreeIdentifiers()
      Returns the set of free identifiers to be substituted.
      Returns:
      the set of free identifiers to be substituted
      Since:
      3.3

    • getPredicateVariables

      PredicateVariable[] getPredicateVariables()
      Returns the set of predicate variables to be substituted.
      Returns:
      the set of predicate variables to be substituted
      Since:
      3.3
